title: epic
description: Enhance the existing team_strategy_score in DriveGradeBreakdown with a comprehensive model that evaluates pit timing, tire compound selection, safety car response, and weather calls. Score is 0-100 with component sub-scores and a decision log for explainability. Uses hindsight simulation, position delta analysis, and peer comparison as ground truth. Pre-computed at ingestion using FastF1 data.

title: Circuit Characteristics
description:
Add comprehensive circuit/track characteristics data to enable comparison and ranking of F1 circuits.

## Data Points Per Circuit
- **Full Throttle %**: Percentage of track spent at full throttle (numeric 1-10 + label)
- **Average Speed**: Circuit average speed in km/h (numeric 1-10 + label)
- **Tire Degradation**: Expected tire wear level (numeric 1-10 + label: Low/Medium/High/Very High)
- **Corner Types**: Count by speed category (slow <100kph, medium 100-180kph, fast >180kph)
- **Downforce Level**: Required aerodynamic downforce (numeric 1-10 + label: Low/Medium/High)
- **Overtaking Difficulty**: How hard it is to pass (numeric 1-10 + label: Easy/Medium/Hard)
- **Track Abrasion**: Surface roughness affecting tires (numeric 1-10 + label)
- **DRS Zones**: Count of DRS activation zones
- **Circuit Type**: Street / Permanent / Hybrid

## Features
- API endpoints for CRUD operations on circuit characteristics
- Comparison API: Compare multiple circuits side-by-side
- Ranking API: Rank circuits by any characteristic
- Frontend UI for comparison and visualization

## Data Source
Research from official F1 telemetry data, Pirelli tire performance reports, FIA technical bulletins, and trusted motorsport sources.

## Technical Notes
- Single current value per circuit (no per-season versioning)
- Numeric scale: 1-10 for all characteristics
- Labels provide human-readable context

title: Create CircuitCharacteristics model + Alembic migration
description:
Create a new CircuitCharacteristics SQLAlchemy model and Alembic migration.

## Model Design (separate table from circuits)
```python
class CircuitCharacteristics(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'circuit_characteristics'
    __table_args__ = {'schema': 'core'}

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    circuit_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('core.circuits.id'), nullable=False)
    effective_year = Column(Integer, nullable=False, default=2024)

    # Performance characteristics
    full_throttle_pct = Column(Float)
    full_throttle_score = Column(Integer)  # 1-10
    average_speed_kph = Column(Float)
    average_speed_score = Column(Integer)  # 1-10
    track_length_km = Column(Float)

    # Tire characteristics
    tire_degradation_score = Column(Integer)  # 1-10
    tire_degradation_label = Column(String(20))
    track_abrasion_score = Column(Integer)  # 1-10
    track_abrasion_label = Column(String(20))

    # Corner profile
    corners_slow = Column(Integer)
    corners_medium = Column(Integer)
    corners_fast = Column(Integer)

    # Aero & racing
    downforce_score = Column(Integer)
    downforce_label = Column(String(20))
    overtaking_difficulty_score = Column(Integer)
    overtaking_difficulty_label = Column(String(20))
    drs_zones = Column(Integer)
    circuit_type = Column(String(20))

    # Metadata
    data_completeness = Column(String(20), default='unknown')
    last_updated = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now())

    __table_args__ = (
        UniqueConstraint('circuit_id', 'effective_year'),
        Index('idx_circuit_chars_circuit_year', 'circuit_id', 'effective_year'),
        {'schema': 'core'}
    )
```

## Tasks
1. Add CircuitCharacteristics model to src/theundercut/models.py
2. Generate migration: alembic revision --autogenerate -m 'add_circuit_characteristics'
3. Add CHECK constraints for score 1-10, valid labels, circuit_type enum
4. Test migration up/down on local DB
5. Create Pydantic schemas for API serialization

## Deployment Plan
- Migration runs BEFORE API deploy
- Rollback: migration includes downgrade() to drop table

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Model created with all fields + constraints
- [ ] Migration runs without errors (up and down)
- [ ] Unique constraint on (circuit_id, effective_year)
- [ ] Pydantic schema validates score ranges

title: Create circuit characteristics API endpoints
description:
Create API endpoints for accessing and managing circuit characteristics.

## Endpoints

### Read Operations
- `GET /api/v1/circuits` - List all circuits with characteristics
- `GET /api/v1/circuits/{circuit_id}` - Get single circuit with all characteristics
- `GET /api/v1/circuits/{circuit_id}/characteristics` - Get just the characteristics data

### Admin Operations (authenticated)
- `PUT /api/v1/circuits/{circuit_id}/characteristics` - Update circuit characteristics
- `PATCH /api/v1/circuits/{circuit_id}/characteristics` - Partial update

## Response Schema
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "name": "Albert Park Circuit",
  "country": "Australia",
  "characteristics": {
    "full_throttle": { "value": 72.5, "score": 7, "label": "High" },
    "average_speed": { "value": 238.5, "score": 8, "label": "Very High" },
    "tire_degradation": { "score": 6, "label": "Medium-High" },
    "track_abrasion": { "score": 5, "label": "Medium" },
    "corners": { "slow": 4, "medium": 6, "fast": 4, "total": 14 },
    "downforce": { "score": 5, "label": "Medium" },
    "overtaking": { "score": 6, "label": "Medium" },
    "drs_zones": 3,
    "circuit_type": "Permanent"
  }
}
```

## Implementation
- Create new router: src/theundercut/api/v1/circuits.py
- Add Pydantic response models
- Cache responses (characteristics rarely change)
- Add OpenAPI documentation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All endpoints return correct data structure
- [ ] Response includes both numeric and label values
- [ ] Caching implemented for read endpoints
- [ ] OpenAPI docs generated

title: Create comparison and ranking API endpoints
description:
Create API endpoints for comparing and ranking circuits by characteristics.

## Comparison Endpoint
`GET /api/v1/circuits/compare?ids=1,5,12`

Compare multiple circuits side-by-side:
```json
{
  "circuits": [
    { "id": 1, "name": "Albert Park", "characteristics": {...} },
    { "id": 5, "name": "Monaco", "characteristics": {...} },
    { "id": 12, "name": "Spa", "characteristics": {...} }
  ],
  "comparison": {
    "highest_full_throttle": { "circuit_id": 12, "value": 75.2 },
    "lowest_overtaking_difficulty": { "circuit_id": 1, "score": 5 },
    "most_corners": { "circuit_id": 5, "total": 19 }
  }
}
```

## Ranking Endpoints
`GET /api/v1/circuits/rank?by=full_throttle_score&order=desc&limit=10`

Rank circuits by any characteristic:
```json
{
  "ranking": [
    { "rank": 1, "circuit_id": 15, "name": "Monza", "value": 80.1, "score": 10 },
    { "rank": 2, "circuit_id": 12, "name": "Spa", "value": 75.2, "score": 9 },
    ...
  ],
  "ranked_by": "full_throttle_score",
  "order": "desc"
}
```

## Supported Ranking Fields
- full_throttle_score, average_speed_score
- tire_degradation_score, track_abrasion_score
- downforce_score, overtaking_difficulty_score
- drs_zones, corners_total (sum of slow+medium+fast)

## Implementation
- Add to circuits router
- Validate query parameters
- Support pagination for rankings
- Cache rankings (bust on characteristic update)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Compare up to 5 circuits at once
- [ ] Rank by any numeric characteristic
- [ ] Support asc/desc ordering
- [ ] Include circuit metadata in response

title: Seed circuit data with researched characteristics
description:
Research and populate circuit characteristics for all current F1 calendar circuits.

## Data Sources
1. **Official F1 telemetry data** - Full throttle %, average speeds
2. **Pirelli tire performance reports** - Degradation levels, abrasion data
3. **FIA technical bulletins** - DRS zones, circuit specifications
4. **Trusted motorsport sources** - f1-tempo.com, racefans.net, formula1.com

## Circuits to Seed (2024-2026 Calendar)
1. Bahrain (Sakhir)
2. Saudi Arabia (Jeddah)
3. Australia (Melbourne)
4. Japan (Suzuka)
5. China (Shanghai)
6. Miami
7. Emilia Romagna (Imola)
8. Monaco
9. Canada (Montreal)
10. Spain (Barcelona)
11. Austria (Spielberg)
12. Great Britain (Silverstone)
13. Hungary (Budapest)
14. Belgium (Spa)
15. Netherlands (Zandvoort)
16. Italy (Monza)
17. Azerbaijan (Baku)
18. Singapore (Marina Bay)
19. USA (Austin - COTA)
20. Mexico (Mexico City)
21. Brazil (Interlagos)
22. Las Vegas
23. Qatar (Lusail)
24. Abu Dhabi (Yas Marina)

## Deliverables
1. Create seed script: scripts/seed_circuit_characteristics.py
2. JSON data file: data/circuit_characteristics.json
3. CLI command: theundercut seed-circuits [--force]

## Data Format Example
```json
{
  "albert_park": {
    "full_throttle_pct": 72.5,
    "average_speed_kph": 238.5,
    "tire_degradation": { "score": 6, "label": "Medium-High" },

    "overtaking_difficulty": { "score": 6, "label": "Medium" },
    "track_abrasion": { "score": 5, "label": "Medium" },
    "drs_zones": 3,
    "circuit_type": "Permanent"
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 24 circuits have complete data
- [ ] Data sourced from reliable references
- [ ] Seed script is idempotent
- [ ] CLI command documented

title: Create frontend circuit comparison UI
description:
Build frontend UI for viewing and comparing circuit characteristics.

## Pages/Components

### Circuit List Page
`/circuits`
- Grid/table view of all circuits
- Sortable columns for each characteristic
- Filter by circuit type (Street/Permanent/Hybrid)
- Click to view circuit detail

### Circuit Detail Page
`/circuits/{circuit_id}`
- Full characteristics display with visual indicators
- Radar chart showing all scores
- Compare button to add to comparison
- Historical race results at this circuit

### Circuit Comparison Page
`/circuits/compare?ids=1,5,12`
- Side-by-side comparison of 2-5 circuits
- Radar chart overlay for visual comparison
- Highlight best/worst in each category
- Share comparison link

### Ranking View
`/circuits/rankings`
- Dropdown to select ranking characteristic
- Bar chart visualization
- Top 5 / Bottom 5 quick views

## Components
```
web/src/components/circuits/
├── CircuitCard.tsx
├── CircuitCharacteristicsDisplay.tsx
├── CircuitRadarChart.tsx
├── CircuitComparisonTable.tsx
├── CircuitRankingChart.tsx
└── index.ts
```

## Design
- Use existing design system (Tailwind + shadcn/ui)
- Color-coded scores (red=low, yellow=medium, green=high)
- Responsive: stack on mobile, grid on desktop
- Dark mode compatible

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List page shows all circuits with key stats
- [ ] Detail page shows complete characteristics
- [ ] Comparison page supports 2-5 circuits
- [ ] Radar chart renders correctly
- [ ] Mobile responsive
- [ ] Accessible (a11y)

title: Unit tests for circuit characteristics API
description:
Comprehensive unit tests for circuit characteristics API endpoints.

## Test Files
- tests/api/test_circuits.py
- tests/api/test_circuits_compare.py
- tests/api/test_circuits_ranking.py

## Test Cases

### GET /circuits
- Returns list of all circuits with characteristics
- Includes all characteristic fields
- Handles empty database gracefully

### GET /circuits/{id}
- Returns correct circuit
- Returns 404 for non-existent circuit
- Includes full characteristics data

### GET /circuits/compare
- Compares 2 circuits correctly
- Compares max 5 circuits
- Returns 400 for >5 circuits
- Returns 404 for invalid circuit ID
- Includes comparison highlights

### GET /circuits/rank
- Ranks by each characteristic
- Supports asc/desc ordering
- Validates ranking field parameter
- Supports limit parameter
- Returns empty for no circuits

### PUT/PATCH /circuits/{id}/characteristics
- Updates characteristics correctly
- Validates score range (1-10)
- Validates label values
- Requires authentication
- Busts cache on update

## Mocking
- Mock database session
- No external API calls

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All endpoints have test coverage
- [ ] Edge cases tested (empty data, invalid params)
- [ ] Authentication tested for admin endpoints
- [ ] Tests run in CI pipeline

title: Enhanced Strategy Score
description: Enhance the existing team_strategy_score in DriveGradeBreakdown with a comprehensive model that evaluates pit timing, tire compound selection, safety car response, and weather calls. Score is 0-100 with component sub-scores and a decision log for explainability. Uses hindsight simulation, position delta analysis, and peer comparison as ground truth. Pre-computed at ingestion using FastF1 data.

title: Create StrategyScore and StrategyDecision models
description:
Create SQLAlchemy models for storing strategy scores and decision logs:
- StrategyScore: entry_id (FK to core.entries), total_score (0-100), pit_timing_score, tire_selection_score, safety_car_score, weather_score, calibration_profile, calibration_version, computed_at
- StrategyDecision: FK to StrategyScore, lap_number, decision_type, factor, impact_score, position_delta, time_delta_ms, explanation, comparison_context
- RaceControlEvent: race_id, event_type (safety_car/vsc/red_flag), start_lap, end_lap, timestamps, cause
- RaceWeather: race_id, lap_number, track_status, temps, humidity, rain_intensity
- LapPosition: race_id, entry_id, lap_number, position, gap_to_leader_ms, gap_to_ahead_ms
- Alembic migration to create all tables with proper FKs and indexes
- Use entry_id FK for referential integrity (not denormalized driver/team)
close_reason: Closed

title: Integrate strategy scoring into ingestion pipeline
description:
Hook strategy score calculation into the existing ingestion pipeline:
- After session/race data is ingested, trigger strategy scoring
- Store results in StrategyScore and StrategyDecision tables
- Update cache invalidation to include strategy scores
- Ensure idempotent re-ingestion (update existing scores)

title: Expose strategy score via API endpoints
description:
Create API endpoints for strategy score access:
- GET /api/v1/strategy/{season}/{round}/scores - All driver scores for a race
- GET /api/v1/strategy/{season}/{round}/driver/{driver} - Single driver detailed breakdown + decision log
- Include strategy_score in existing /analytics endpoint response
- Add OpenAPI documentation

title: Unit tests for strategy factor scorers
description:
Comprehensive unit tests for each factor scorer:
- PitTimingScorer: undercut detection, pit window timing, edge cases
- TireSelectionScorer: compound choices, stint lengths, sequences
- SafetyCarScorer: SC detection, pit decisions, position impact
- WeatherScorer: transition detection, timing evaluation, dry races
- Use pytest fixtures with realistic race scenarios

title: Unit tests for simulation and comparison engines
description:
Unit tests for supporting engines:
- HindsightSimulator: position projections, traffic modeling
- PositionDeltaAnalyzer: attribution, pit stop tracking
- PeerComparison: grouping logic, percentile calculations
- StrategyScoreEngine: orchestration, score combination

title: E2E tests for strategy score API
description:
End-to-end tests for strategy score feature:
- Test full flow: ingestion -> scoring -> API response
- Verify score breakdown structure matches schema
- Test decision log contains expected events
- Test error handling for races without strategy data
- Test caching behavior

title: Add strategy score calibration to CalibrationProfile
description:
Extend the existing CalibrationProfile with strategy scoring parameters:
- Weights for each factor (pit, tire, SC, weather)
- Thresholds for undercut/overcut detection
- Position delta attribution rules
- Peer grouping criteria (pace delta threshold)
- Allow JSON-based calibration like existing Drive Grade

title: Ingest SC/VSC periods from FastF1 race control messages
description:
Extract Safety Car and VSC periods from FastF1 data:
- Parse session.race_control_messages for SC, VSC, RED FLAG events
- Create race_control_events table (see updated schema in DESIGN doc)
- Persist start_lap, end_lap, event_type, timestamps
- Add to ingestion pipeline after race data load
- Required for SafetyCar scorer to function
close_reason: Closed

title: Ingest weather data from FastF1 weather_data
description:
Extract per-lap weather conditions from FastF1:
- Parse session.weather_data for track status, temps, humidity, rain
- Create race_weather table (see updated schema in DESIGN doc)
- Persist lap-by-lap weather snapshots
- Add to ingestion pipeline after race data load
- Required for Weather scorer to function
close_reason: Closed

title: Ingest per-lap position data
description:
Extract or derive per-lap position snapshots:
- Parse FastF1 lap data for position, gap_to_leader, gap_to_ahead
- Create lap_positions table (see updated schema in DESIGN doc)
- Derive positions from cumulative lap times if direct data unavailable
- Add to ingestion pipeline after lap data load
- Required for Position Delta Analyzer and Pit Timing Scorer
close_reason: Closed

title: Add recompute/backfill CLI commands
description:
Add CLI commands for retroactive score computation:
- strategy-score recompute --season N [--round R]
- strategy-score backfill --seasons X,Y,Z
- Uses idempotent upsert strategy
- Respects current calibration profile
- Logs progress and handles errors gracefully

title: Implement pit timing factor scorer
description:
Implement pit timing evaluation logic:
- Detect undercut/overcut attempts and their success
- Evaluate pit window timing (too early, optimal, too late)
- Analyze reaction time to gaps opening in traffic
- Compare pit delta to expected pit loss
- Output: PitTimingScore (0-100) + list of PitDecision events

title: Implement tire compound selection scorer
description:
Implement tire compound selection evaluation:
- Analyze compound choice vs track conditions (temp, degradation)
- Compare stint length achieved vs expected for compound
- Evaluate starting tire strategy (start compound selection)
- Detect suboptimal compound sequences (e.g., H->M when M->H was better)
- Output: TireSelectionScore (0-100) + list of TireDecision events

title: Implement safety car response scorer
description:
Implement safety car/VSC response evaluation:
- Detect SC/VSC periods from FastF1 data
- Analyze pit decisions during SC windows (pit vs stay out)
- Calculate position delta from SC strategy choices
- Evaluate timing of pit call relative to SC queue formation
- Output: SafetyCarScore (0-100) + list of SCDecision events

title: Implement weather response scorer
description:
Implement weather-related strategy evaluation:
- Detect weather transitions (dry->wet, wet->dry) from session data
- Analyze timing of intermediate/wet tire switches
- Compare call timing to field average
- Evaluate staying out on wrong tires vs early/late switch
- Handle races with no weather changes (neutral score)
- Output: WeatherScore (0-100) + list of WeatherDecision events

title: Build hindsight simulation engine
description:
Implement hindsight simulation for strategy evaluation:
- Given actual race data, simulate alternative strategy choices
- Model position changes from different pit timing
- Account for traffic, undercuts, and tire degradation curves
- Provide 'what-if' position projections
- Used by factor scorers to determine optimal vs actual gap

title: Build position delta analyzer
description:
Implement position delta analysis for strategic decisions:
- Track position changes around each pit stop
- Identify undercut victims and overcut successes
- Calculate net position gain/loss per strategic decision
- Attribute position changes to strategy vs pace
- Feeds into factor scorers for impact calculation

title: Build peer comparison logic
description:
Implement field comparison for strategy evaluation:
- Group similar-pace cars for fair comparison
- Compare pit timing decisions to peers
- Analyze who reacted fastest to SC/weather
- Calculate percentile ranking for each factor
- Provides relative context for absolute scores

title: Create StrategyScoreEngine orchestrator
description:
Create central orchestrator class that:
- Coordinates all factor scorers (pit, tire, SC, weather)
- Uses simulation engine and position analyzer as inputs
- Applies peer comparison for relative adjustments
- Combines component scores into total 0-100 score
- Generates decision log with explanations
- Replaces current simple compute_strategy_score()

title: Race Weekend Widget
description:
Dynamic homepage widget that shows upcoming race info and updates as the weekend unfolds with practice, qualifying, sprint (if applicable), and race results.

## Requirements
- Shows next race countdown (even during off-weeks)
- Displays historical data: last year's winner and podium
- Progressive reveal: compact summary (top 3) → full grid on expand
- Replaces existing 'Last Race Results' widget
- Sessions appear as separate cards as they complete (FP1, FP2, FP3, Quali, Sprint, Race)
- Sprint weekends: sprint quali and sprint race shown as regular sessions
- No live timing (results only after session completes)
- ISR revalidation (5 min) for data freshness

## Key Gaps to Fill
1. API endpoint for race weekend schedule with session times
2. API endpoints for practice/qualifying/sprint session results
3. Frontend types for race weekend data
4. Historical race data (previous year's results)
close_reason: Race Weekend Widget feature complete: backend API, frontend components, and full test coverage (7 backend + 9 E2E + 55 unit tests)

title: Create race weekend schedule API endpoint
description:
Create new API endpoint to expose race weekend schedule with session times.

## Endpoint
`GET /api/v1/race/{season}/{round}/schedule`

## Response
- List of sessions: FP1, FP2, FP3, Qualifying, Sprint Quali (if applicable), Sprint Race (if applicable), Race
- Each session includes: session_type, start_time (UTC), end_time (UTC), status (scheduled/completed)
- Include race metadata: race_name, circuit_name, circuit_country

## Implementation
- Leverage existing CalendarEvent model which has this data
- Query by season + round to get all sessions for that race weekend
- Handle sprint vs non-sprint weekend formats

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Endpoint returns all sessions for a race weekend
- [ ] Session times are in UTC with proper timezone handling
- [ ] Status reflects whether session is scheduled or completed
- [ ] Sprint weekends include sprint-specific sessions

title: Create SessionClassification model + Alembic migration
description:
Create the SessionClassification database model and generate Alembic migration.

## Why This Is Needed (Codex Design Review)
The original design had a CRITICAL flaw: dual source of truth. Widget would fetch from external APIs while the rest of the product uses Postgres. This creates inconsistency and availability issues.

## Model
```python
class SessionClassification(Base):
    __tablename__ = 'session_classifications'
    
    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
    season = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    round = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    session_type = Column(String(20), nullable=False)
    driver_code = Column(String(3), nullable=False)
    driver_name = Column(String(100))
    team = Column(String(50))
    position = Column(Integer)
    time_ms = Column(Float)
    gap_ms = Column(Float)
    laps = Column(Integer)
    points = Column(Integer)
    q1_time_ms = Column(Float)
    q2_time_ms = Column(Float)
    q3_time_ms = Column(Float)
    eliminated_in = Column(String(5))
    ingested_at = Column(DateTime, default=func.now())
    amended = Column(Boolean, default=False)
```

## Tasks
1. Add model to src/theundercut/models.py
2. Generate migration: alembic revision --autogenerate -m 'add_session_classifications'
3. Test migration on local DB
4. Add to indexes for (season, round, session_type) lookups

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Model created with all fields
- [ ] Migration runs without errors
- [ ] Unique constraint on (season, round, session_type, driver_code)
- [ ] Index on (season, round, session_type)

title: Extend ingestion to persist session classifications
description:
Extend the existing ingestion pipeline to fetch and persist session classifications after each session completes.

## Why This Is Needed (Codex Design Review)
Without this, the widget depends on external APIs being available. By persisting classifications during ingestion, we have a single source of truth shared with the rest of the product.

## Implementation
Modify `src/theundercut/services/ingestion.py`:

1. After session ingestion completes (laps/stints stored):
   - Fetch session classifications from FastF1 (`session.results`)
   - Map to SessionClassification model
   - Bulk insert with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE (for penalty amendments)

2. For qualifying sessions:
   - Extract Q1/Q2/Q3 times from FastF1 session data
   - Populate eliminated_in field based on classification

3. For race/sprint:
   - Include points awarded
   - Handle DNF/DSQ classifications

## Data Source
FastF1 provides `session.results` DataFrame with:
- Position, DriverNumber, Abbreviation, TeamName
- Time (for race), Q1/Q2/Q3 (for quali), BestLapTime (for practice)
- Points (for race/sprint)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Practice sessions persist best lap + gap + total laps
- [ ] Qualifying persists Q1/Q2/Q3 times + elimination stage
- [ ] Race/Sprint persists classification + points
- [ ] ON CONFLICT updates existing rows (for penalties)
- [ ] Sets amended=True when updating existing classification

title: Add cache invalidation hooks tied to ingestion
description:
Add Redis cache invalidation hooks that trigger when ingestion completes or classifications are amended.

## Why This Is Needed (Codex Design Review)
The original design used fixed TTLs (24h for completed sessions) which could serve stale data after post-race penalties. Cache must be invalidated by events, not time.

## Implementation

1. **Ingestion completion hook** (in services/ingestion.py):
   ```python
   def _bust_session_cache(season: int, round: int, session_type: str):
       redis_client.delete(f'session:{season}:{round}:{session_type}')
       redis_client.delete(f'weekend:{season}:{round}')  # Aggregated endpoint
   ```

2. **CalendarEvent status change** (when status -> 'ingested'):
   - Bust schedule cache: `schedule:{season}:{round}`
   - Bust session cache for that session type

3. **Penalty/amendment detection**:
   - If classification differs from stored, set amended=True
   - Bust cache and log amendment

## Cache Keys Affected
- `schedule:{season}:{round}`
- `session:{season}:{round}:{session_type}`
- `weekend:{season}:{round}` (aggregated endpoint)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cache busted on successful ingestion
- [ ] Cache busted on CalendarEvent.status change
- [ ] Cache busted on classification amendment
- [ ] Logging for cache invalidation events

title: Create aggregated /weekend endpoint
description:
Create a single aggregated endpoint that returns schedule, history, and all session results in one call.

## Why This Is Needed (Codex Design Review)
The original design required 3-8 API calls per render (schedule + history + N session results). This creates latency issues and fragile error handling. A single aggregator is more efficient and robust.

## Endpoint
`GET /api/v1/race/{season}/{round}/weekend`

## Response
```json
{
  "schedule": {
    "season": 2026,
    "round": 3,
    "race_name": "Australian Grand Prix",
    "circuit_id": "albert_park",
    "is_sprint_weekend": false,
    "sessions": [...]
  },
  "history": {
    "previous_year": {...}
  },
  "sessions": {
    "fp1": { "status": "completed", "results": [...] },
    "fp2": { "status": "completed", "results": [...] },
    "qualifying": { "status": "scheduled", "results": null }
  },
  "meta": {
    "last_updated": "2026-03-14T06:15:00Z",
    "stale": false,
    "errors": []
  }
}
```

## Implementation
1. Query CalendarEvent for schedule
2. Query SessionClassification for all session results (single query)
3. Fetch history from Jolpica (cached)
4. Assemble response with per-section error metadata

## Error Handling
- If schedule fetch fails: return 500
- If history fetch fails: return history: null with error in meta.errors
- If some sessions missing: return available sessions with partial flag

## Caching
- Cache key: `weekend:{season}:{round}`
- TTL: 5 min (but invalidated by ingestion hooks)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single endpoint returns all widget data
- [ ] Graceful degradation per section
- [ ] Cached with event-driven invalidation
- [ ] Stale indicator in meta

title: Create session results API endpoints
description:
Create API endpoints to fetch results for any session type (practice, qualifying, sprint).

## Endpoints
`GET /api/v1/race/{season}/{round}/session/{session_type}/results`

Where session_type is: fp1, fp2, fp3, qualifying, sprint_qualifying, sprint_race

## Response
- Ordered list of drivers with position, driver code, team, time/gap
- For qualifying: include Q1/Q2/Q3 times and elimination info
- For practice: include best lap time, laps completed, gap to leader
- For sprint: include positions gained, points awarded

## Implementation
- Use FastF1/OpenF1 data sources for session results
- Cache results in Redis (sessions are immutable once complete)
- Return 404 if session hasn't occurred yet

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All session types return consistent response format
- [ ] Qualifying shows all three sessions (Q1/Q2/Q3)
- [ ] Practice shows best lap times and gap analysis
- [ ] Sprint sessions return grid and race results

title: Create historical race data API endpoint
description:
Create API endpoint to fetch historical results for a specific circuit.

## Endpoint
`GET /api/v1/circuits/{season}/{circuit_id}/history`

Or extend existing circuit endpoint with history parameter.

## Response
- Previous year's race results at this circuit
- Winner, 2nd place, 3rd place with driver name and team
- Pole position holder
- Fastest lap holder

## Implementation
- Use Ergast/Jolpica API for historical data
- Cache aggressively (historical data doesn't change)
- Handle circuits that are new to calendar (no history)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Returns previous year's podium for the circuit
- [ ] Handles new circuits gracefully (empty/null history)
- [ ] Data is cached for performance

title: Add TypeScript types for race weekend data
close_reason: TypeScript types added in web/src/types/api.ts (WeekendResponse, SessionResult, etc.)

title: Create RaceWeekendWidget component
description:
Create the main React component for displaying race weekend information on the homepage.

## Component Structure

```
RaceWeekendWidget/
├── RaceWeekendWidget.tsx      # Main container
├── RaceCountdown.tsx          # Countdown timer to next session
├── SessionCard.tsx            # Individual session result card
├── HistoricalData.tsx         # Previous year's podium
└── index.ts                   # Exports
```

## States to Handle

1. **Pre-weekend**: Show race info + countdown + historical data
2. **During weekend**: Show completed sessions + upcoming session countdown
3. **Post-race**: Show all session results with race as primary
4. **Off-week**: Show next race countdown + next race info

## Features

### Progressive Reveal
- Compact view: Top 3 finishers with times/gaps
- Expanded view: Full session grid (click to expand)
- Use Radix Collapsible or similar

### Session Cards
- Show session type badge (FP1, QUALI, RACE, etc.)
- Status indicator (upcoming/completed)
- Results summary in compact mode
- Full results in expanded mode

### Countdown Timer
- Days/hours/minutes until next session
- Updates on page refresh (no client-side timer needed due to ISR)

### Historical Section
- 'Last Year' badge
- Winner, podium, pole position
- Only show if circuit has history

## Styling
- Match existing design system (Tailwind + shadcn/ui)
- Responsive: stack on mobile, grid on desktop
- Dark mode compatible

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Component renders all race weekend states
- [ ] Progressive reveal works (compact → expanded)
- [ ] Historical data displays when available
- [ ] Responsive design works on mobile/desktop
- [ ] Loading and error states handled
close_reason: RaceWeekendWidget component created in web/src/components/race-weekend/ with all sub-components

title: Replace Last Race Results with RaceWeekendWidget
description:
Update the homepage to use the new RaceWeekendWidget instead of the existing Last Race Results display.

## Changes Required

### web/src/app/(main)/page.tsx
- Remove Last Race Results card rendering
- Import and render RaceWeekendWidget
- Pass required props: season, current round, schedule data

### Data Fetching
- Fetch race weekend schedule in getStaticProps/generateStaticParams
- Determine which round is 'current' (next upcoming or most recent)
- Fetch historical data for the circuit

### Layout Updates
- RaceWeekendWidget should be prominent (top of content area)
- Keep championship standings below
- Pre-season testing widget logic remains (shows when racesCompleted === 0)

### Conditional Logic
- If before first race: show pre-season testing OR first race countdown
- If during season: show current race weekend
- If after last race: show season finale results

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Homepage renders RaceWeekendWidget
- [ ] Last Race Results code removed
- [ ] Pre-season logic still works
- [ ] ISR revalidation configured (5 min)
- [ ] No visual regressions on other homepage elements
close_reason: Homepage updated to use RaceWeekendWidget in web/src/app/(main)/page.tsx

title: Unit tests for race weekend API endpoints

Write unit tests for the new race weekend API endpoints.

## Test Files

## Test Cases

### Schedule Endpoint
- Returns correct sessions for a standard weekend
- Returns correct sessions for a sprint weekend
- Returns 404 for invalid season/round
- Session times are in UTC
- Status reflects completion state

### Session Results Endpoint
- Returns results for completed sessions
- Returns 404 for future sessions
- Qualifying shows Q1/Q2/Q3 breakdown
- Practice shows gap to leader
- Sprint shows points awarded

### Historical Data Endpoint
- Returns previous year's podium
- Returns null for new circuits
- Handles circuits with name changes

## Mocking
- Mock FastF1/OpenF1 responses
- Mock Redis cache for isolation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All endpoints have test coverage
- [ ] Edge cases tested (sprint weekends, new circuits)
- [ ] Tests run in CI pipeline
- [ ] No external API calls in tests

title: E2E tests for race weekend widget

2. Verify countdown to next race shows

### Mobile Responsiveness

2. Verify layout adapts correctly
3. Verify touch interactions work

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tests run in CI
- [ ] Mobile and desktop viewports tested
- [ ] No flaky tests

title: Live Session Auto-Refresh
description:
Show meaningful status during live F1 sessions. Trigger ingestion at session boundaries (start and end) rather than continuous polling.

## Simplified Scope
- Backend: Add 'live' status to CalendarEvent
- Backend: Schedule job at session start to set status to 'live'
- Frontend: Show 'In Progress' indicator for live sessions
- Existing end-of-session ingestion remains unchanged

## What We're NOT Building
- Real-time position updates
- Continuous backend polling
- Sub-minute data freshness
- WebSockets or push notifications

## Design Doc
docs/DESIGN-live-session-auto-refresh.md
close_reason: Live session boundary ingestion implemented - sessions marked as 'live' at start, frontend shows 'In Progress' indicator

title: Add 'live' status to CalendarEvent state machine
description:
Extend CalendarEvent.status to include a 'live' state.

## Current state machine
scheduled → running (when job enqueued post-session) → ingested

## New state machine
scheduled → live (when start_ts reached) → running → ingested

## Implementation
- Add 'live' to status enum/allowed values
- Update enqueue_upcoming() to detect live sessions
- Set status='live' when now >= start_ts AND now < end_ts
- Transition to 'running' when first post-session ingestion enqueued

## Files
- src/theundercut/models.py (CalendarEvent.status)
- src/theundercut/scheduler.py (enqueue_upcoming)
close_reason: Added 'live' status support to frontend type and SessionCard StatusBadge

title: Schedule session start job to set 'live' status
description:
Add a scheduled job that runs at each session's start_ts to:
1. Set CalendarEvent.status = 'live'
2. Invalidate race weekend cache

This is a simple one-shot job, not periodic polling.
close_reason: Added mark_sessions_live() scheduler job that runs every minute to detect session starts

title: Handle partial session data from FastF1 during live sessions
description:
Ensure ingestion handles incomplete/in-progress data gracefully.

## Considerations
- FastF1 may return partial results (not all drivers finished)
- Lap counts will be incomplete
- Times may be provisional
- Some drivers may have 'DNF' or 'PIT' status

## Implementation
- In _store_session_classifications(), handle missing fields
- Allow position=None for drivers still racing
- Add 'provisional' field to SessionClassification? Or infer from session status
- Don't fail ingestion if data is incomplete
- Log warnings for missing expected data

## Testing
- Mock FastF1 response with partial data
- Verify ingestion completes without errors
- Verify UI handles provisional data

## Files
- src/theundercut/services/ingestion.py
- src/theundercut/models.py (optional: add provisional field)
close_reason: Not needed - simplified design doesn't require partial data handling

title: Update frontend to show 'In Progress' for live sessions
description:
Update SessionCard component to display 'In Progress' indicator when session.status === 'live'.

Simple status check from API response - no complex client-side detection needed.
close_reason: SessionCard StatusBadge now shows 'Live' for both 'live' and 'running' statuses

title: Add auto-refresh polling during live sessions
description:
Enable React Query to auto-refetch weekend data when a session is live.

## Requirements
- Poll /api/v1/race/{season}/{round}/weekend every 30-60 seconds
- Only poll when isLive or isImminent is true
- Stop polling when session ends
- Handle errors gracefully (don't spam on failure)

## Implementation
- In page.tsx or data fetching layer, use refetchInterval option
- refetchInterval: isLive ? 30_000 : (isImminent ? 60_000 : false)
- Use React Query's refetchOnWindowFocus: true for tab switches
- Consider exponential backoff on errors

## Files
- web/src/app/(main)/page.tsx (or wherever weekend data is fetched)
- web/src/lib/api.ts (if fetch functions need updating)
close_reason: Not needed - simplified design uses session boundary ingestion, not continuous polling

title: Add live session visual indicator
description:
Show visual feedback when viewing live session data.

## Requirements
- 'LIVE' badge on session card when session is in progress
- Subtle 'Refreshing...' indicator during refetch
- Pulse animation or similar for live state
- 'Last updated X seconds ago' timestamp

## Implementation
- Add LiveBadge component with pulsing dot animation
- Use React Query's isFetching state for refresh indicator
- Show last_updated from meta in WeekendResponse
- Style consistently with existing status badges

## Design
- Red pulsing dot + 'LIVE' text for in-progress session
- Subtle spinner or fade for background refresh
- Gray timestamp for 'Updated 30s ago'

## Files
- web/src/components/race-weekend/SessionCard.tsx (live badge)
- web/src/components/race-weekend/RaceWeekendWidget.tsx (refresh indicator)
- web/src/app/globals.css (animations)
close_reason: Merged into .4 - single task for live indicator
